issue: Per-channel acked cumulative is in-memory only; a gateway restart resets it to 0, so the next voucher re-signs cum=0 < operator-served and the operator rejects it with 409 stale_voucher, permanently bricking the channel.
action: Journal each channel's acked to SURPLUS_GATEWAY_STATE (atomic tmp+rename) on every advance; seed acked from the journal on startup.
verification: gateway-multilot-e2e restarts the gateway mid-run and asserts a post-restart call succeeds + bills further on-chain (settled2b > settled2a).

issue: Serve-auth reservation (used_auths.insert) and served counter are journaled only AFTER inference; a crash mid-serve loses the reservation/served, re-opening quota the operator already spent compute on and can never settle past qtyTokens.
action: persist_redeem() immediately after reserving the auth (before inference) and on release_auth, in addition to the existing post-serve persist.
verification: settlement-e2e redemption serve+receipt path still passes; reservation now durable before the inference call.

issue: The multi-lot gateway e2e only exercised the buffered path; the streaming path through the gateway (SSE tee + surplus-event strip + trailing ack) was untested.
action: Add a stream:true request through the gateway, assert SSE content flows and the private surplus event is stripped.
verification: gateway-multilot-e2e: 'streamed N chars through the gateway (surplus event stripped)'.

issue: The consumption money-paths (spend-e2e.sh, gateway-multilot-e2e.sh) were excluded from CI; only the settlement spine e2e ran, so a regression in the spend rail / voucher metering / multi-lot routing / streaming / gateway persistence would ship unnoticed.
action: Add a money-e2e CI job (rust+foundry+node+pnpm) running spend-e2e.sh and gateway-multilot-e2e.sh; declare viem as a root devDependency so node resolves it after pnpm install.
verification: ci.yml jobs now include money-e2e; both scripts pass locally end-to-end.

issue: The app CI job was typecheck-only; the SOR math (planRoute/nbboTouch in router.check.ts) ran nowhere, so a routing-math regression was uncaught.
action: Add tsx + a test script to app/package.json running router.check.ts; add an 'app — test' CI step.
verification: pnpm test in app prints 'router: 15 passed, 0 failed'; CI step added.

issue: authorize() never mirrors on-chain revocation (spendRevoked) nor the lot's current holder, contradicting spend-rail.md: a leaked/revoked session key or a resold lot keeps being SERVED off-chain even though settleSpend would revert — the operator gives away free, unbillable inference.
action: reconcile_revocations() on each flush: query spend_revoked(permitDigest) + get_lot(lotId).holder for every channel and drop revoked/resold ones (fail-open on transient RPC error); doc corrected to describe the flush-cycle bound.
verification: spend-e2e revokes on-chain, asserts flush reports dropped>=1 and a subsequent serve returns 401 with no further billing.

issue: complete_stream spawns a detached task with no timeout/abort on client disconnect — a stuck upstream leaks the task and the SSE channel.
action: Bound the spawned stream task with a timeout / abort-on-drop (follow-up).
verification: stream task terminates on client disconnect within the bound.

issue: redeem_serve does not assert_domain before computing receipt digests, unlike the CLOB/spend flush paths; a wrong chain-id/contract would produce unverifiable receipts.
action: Call assert_domain once in the redeem serve path (follow-up).
verification: redeem serve fails closed on domain drift.

issue: redeem_attest_action can re-attest in a loop after challengeAttested, draining gas.
action: Add backoff/terminal state after a challenge (follow-up).
verification: attest pump stops re-attesting a challenged redemption.

issue: The SOR silently drops a sub-leg when the operator is not in the venues set, under-filling without surfacing it.
action: Surface dropped legs to the user or fail the route (follow-up).
verification: a route with an unknown-venue leg reports the shortfall.

issue: BOOK_THRESHOLD defaults to 1; a value chain could silently launch a trivial 1-of-1 book.
action: Guard/require explicit threshold on a production deploy (follow-up; testnet-release.md documents 1-of-1 launch intent).
verification: deploy refuses an unspecified threshold on a non-dev chain.

issue: redeem serve cap (max_tokens.unwrap_or(1024)) differs from the digest's max_tokens basis; potential mismatch.
action: Align the cap and the signed digest basis (follow-up).
verification: served cap equals the authorized digest bound.

issue: Requires SURPLUS_SHIELDED_CREDITS_ADDRESS but never uses it (dead require / shielded not wired).
action: Drop the dead require or wire the value (follow-up).
verification: base-sepolia.sh has no unused required env.

issue: Concurrent requests on the same channel can double-serve before acked advances (no per-channel in-flight gate).
action: Serialize or reserve per-channel in-flight budget (follow-up).
verification: concurrent calls on one channel never exceed the cap.
deploy aborts on a malformed address.
